hook.text: A misconfigured K8s dashboard gave anonymous internet access. Attackers deployed crypto miners.

buildup.text: K8s has many security surfaces: API server, etcd, kubelet, pod specs, network policies, and RBAC.

discovery.text: RBAC controls who can do what. Limit service accounts. Network policies restrict pod-to-pod traffic.

twist.text: Pod Security Standards block root, host networking, and privilege escalation. Apply them cluster-wide.

climax.text: kube-bench and Polaris audit clusters against CIS benchmarks and best practices automatically.

punchline.text: Lock down K8s layer by layer. RBAC, network, pod security.

quiz.question: What do Kubernetes network policies control?
options: Which pods can communicate with each other over the network
How much CPU each pod can use
The programming language used inside pods
